+      border-right: 1px solid var(--line);
+      border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line);
+      line-height: 1.55;
+      background: rgba(255,255,255,0.9);
+    }
+    .cell.head {
+      background: #efe8dc;
+      font-weight: 700;
+    }
+    .cell.side {
+      background: #f5f0e8;
+      font-weight: 700;
+    }
+    .fusion {
+      display: grid;
+      grid-template-columns: repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr));
+      gap: 16px;
+      margin-bottom: 18px;
+    }
+    .fusion-col {
+      background: var(--card);
+      border: 1px solid var(--line);
+      border-radius: 18px;
+      padding: 16px;
+    }
+    .fusion-col h3 {
+      margin: 0 0 10px;
+      font-size: 19px;
+    }
+    .borrow {
+      margin-top: 12px;
+      padding: 10px 12px;
+      border-radius: 12px;
+      font-size: 14px;
+      line-height: 1.55;
+    }
+    .borrow.keep { background: #e7f4ea; color: #225a33; }
+    .borrow.deer { background: #e8f0ff; color: #1d4f7c; }
+    .borrow.miro { background: #f8efe3; color: #77571d; }
+    .flow-grid {
+      display: grid;
+      grid-template-columns: repeat(10, minmax(0, 1fr));
+      gap: 10px;
+      align-items: stretch;
+    }
+    .step {
+      background: var(--card);
+      border: 1px solid var(--line);
+      border-radius: 14px;
+      padding: 12px 10px;
+      min-height: 108px;
+      font-size: 14px;
+      line-height: 1.55;
+    }
+    .step strong {
+      display: block;
+      margin-bottom: 4px;
+      color: #1f2f42;
+    }
+    .callout-row {
+      display: grid;
+      grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr));
+      gap: 14px;
+      margin-top: 16px;
+    }
+    .callout {
+      border-radius: 18px;
+      padding: 16px;
+      border: 1px solid var(--line);
+      background: var(--card);
+    }
+    .callout h4 {
+      margin: 0 0 8px;
+      font-size: 18px;
+    }
+    ul.flat {
+      margin: 8px 0 0 18px;
+      padding: 0;
+      color: var(--muted);
+      line-height: 1.7;
+    }
+    .footer {
+      margin-top: 8px;
+      color: var(--muted);
+      font-size: 13px;
+      line-height: 1.6;
+    }
+    @media (max-width: 1200px) {
+      .three-col, .fusion, .callout-row, .macro-flow, .flow-grid, .matrix {
+        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
+      }
+      .arrow { display: none; }
+    }
+  </style>
+</head>
+<body>
+  <div class="page">
+    <div class="hero">
+      <div class="pill-row">
+        <span class="pill">MiroFish = vertical product shell</span>
+        <span class="pill">DeerFlow = horizontal agent runtime</span>
+        <span class="pill">OpenFons = source-native knowledge factory</span>
+      </div>
+      <h1>Three Architectures, One Fusion Direction</h1>
+      <p>
+        这张页的目标不是简单比较谁“更高级”，而是把三者拆成可借鉴的层：
+        哪些属于控制层，哪些属于执行层，哪些属于沉淀层，哪些属于交付层。
+        最终目的是得到一个既可扩展、又解耦、还能沉淀长期资产的 OpenFons 最佳实践架构。
+      </p>
+    </div>
+
+    <div class="section">
+      <h2>1. Macro Logic: They Are the Same Family</h2>
+      <p class="lead">从大逻辑看，三个项目都属于同一种系统范式：用户输入问题，AI 拆解任务，调用工具/检索/采集，最后输出报告或可视化结果。真正拉开差距的，是中间产物是否可沉淀、是否有机会判断门禁、以及执行层是否足够解耦。</p>
+      <div class="macro-flow">
+        <div class="flow-box">User Input</div>
+        <div class="arrow">-&gt;</div>
+        <div class="flow-box">AI Understanding and Task Decomposition</div>
+        <div class="arrow">-&gt;</div>
+        <div class="flow-box">Skills / Search / Collection / Tools</div>
+        <div class="arrow">-&gt;</div>
+        <div class="flow-box">Analysis / Synthesis / Report / Visualization</div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+
+    <div class="section">
+      <h2>2. Three Project Architectures</h2>
+      <p class="lead">这三列不是按代码量分，而是按“系统重心”分。你会看到：MiroFish 重在产品表达，DeerFlow 重在运行时组织，OpenFons 的目标则应该重在控制层 + 沉淀层。</p>
+      <div class="three-col">
+        <div class="project">
+          <span class="tag product">MiroFish</span>
+          <h3>MiroFish</h3>
+          <div class="stack">
+            <div class="layer l1"><strong>Entry and UX</strong><br>Upload material, prompt demand, guided product flow, report-oriented UI.</div>
+            <div class="layer l2"><strong>Product Orchestration</strong><br>Ontology generation, graph building, simulation setup, report generation.</div>
+            <div class="layer l3"><strong>Execution and Data Use</strong><br>Call LLM services, graph memory, simulation runner, reporting pipeline.</div>
+            <div class="layer l4"><strong>Output</strong><br>Knowledge graph, simulation result, report page, interaction view.</div>
+          </div>
+          <div class="notes">
+            <div class="note">Strength: strongest outward product story and visual delivery.</div>
+            <div class="note">Weakness: not ideal as a reusable general execution substrate.</div>
+            <div class="note">Best borrowing target: report-web, process visualization, narrative packaging.</div>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+
+        <div class="project">
+          <span class="tag runtime">DeerFlow</span>
+          <h3>DeerFlow</h3>
+          <div class="stack">
+            <div class="layer l1"><strong>Entry and Control Surface</strong><br>Frontend console, gateway, config, LangGraph thread/run APIs.</div>
+            <div class="layer l2"><strong>Runtime Harness</strong><br>Lead agent, skills loader, tools registry, subagent executor, memory prompts.</div>
+            <div class="layer l3"><strong>Provider and Tool Layer</strong><br>Model adapters, credential loader, search/tools, memory services, execution logic.</div>
+            <div class="layer l4"><strong>Output</strong><br>Agent run results, streamed execution, intermediate runtime state.</div>
+          </div>
+          <div class="notes">
+            <div class="note">Strength: strongest execution-layer modularity and runtime organization.</div>
+            <div class="note">Weakness: more focused on getting a task done than on long-term evidence assetization.</div>
+            <div class="note">Best borrowing target: harness, skills, provider abstraction, subagent runtime.</div>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+
+        <div class="project">
+          <span class="tag platform">OpenFons Goal</span>
+          <h3>OpenFons</h3>
+          <div class="stack">
+            <div class="layer l1"><strong>Access Plane</strong><br>User input from IM, Web, API, or operator workflows.</div>
+            <div class="layer l2"><strong>Control Plane</strong><br>Intent Structuring, Planning Swarm, Opportunity Judge, OpportunitySpec, Task Compiler.</div>
+            <div class="layer l3"><strong>Execution Plane</strong><br>Skills, workers, controlled collection, source registry, policy gates.</div>
+            <div class="layer l4"><strong>Data and Delivery Plane</strong><br>Topic, Evidence, EvidenceSet, Artifact, ReportSpec, report-web.</div>
+          </div>
+          <div class="notes">
+            <div class="note">Core difference: execution is not the final goal; evidence and artifacts are first-class assets.</div>
+            <div class="note">Core risk today: execution-layer modularity and product-level delivery experience are both not yet mature enough.</div>
+            <div class="note">Core mission: keep the control plane, strengthen runtime, and elevate delivery.</div>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+
+    <div class="section">
+      <h2>3. What Each One Is Really Best At</h2>
+      <p class="lead">这部分回答一个关键问题：如果不把系统拆层，只讲“整体谁更好”，很容易失真。正确问法应该是“哪一层借谁”。</p>
+      <div class="matrix">
+        <div class="cell head"></div>
+        <div class="cell head">MiroFish</div>
+        <div class="cell head">DeerFlow</div>
+        <div class="cell head">OpenFons Should Become</div>
+
+        <div class="cell side">Best Role</div>
+        <div class="cell">Vertical product shell</div>
+        <div class="cell">Horizontal execution runtime</div>
+        <div class="cell">Source-native research and publishing platform</div>
+
+        <div class="cell side">Strongest Layer</div>
+        <div class="cell">Delivery and product presentation</div>
+        <div class="cell">Execution and orchestration</div>
+        <div class="cell">Control plane plus long-term evidence assetization</div>
+
+        <div class="cell side">Weakest Layer</div>
+        <div class="cell">General-purpose decoupled execution substrate</div>
+        <div class="cell">Evidence-first control and asset persistence</div>
+        <div class="cell">Runtime modularity and mature productized output UX</div>
+
+        <div class="cell side">Borrow Target</div>
+        <div class="cell">Report UX, graph/report narrative, process visibility</div>
+        <div class="cell">Skills, providers, subagents, gateway, harness</div>
+        <div class="cell">Keep OpportunitySpec, EvidenceSet, ReportSpec as SoT</div>
+
+        <div class="cell side">Not To Copy</div>
+        <div class="cell">AGPL code path and simulation-heavy core flow</div>
+        <div class="cell">Runtime state as final source of truth</div>
+        <div class="cell">Do not collapse into one giant all-purpose agent</div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+
+    <div class="section">
+      <h2>4. Fusion Architecture: Recommended Best Practice</h2>
+      <p class="lead">最优融合方案不是“把两个仓库拼起来”，而是分层取长补短。控制层保留 OpenFons 自己的灵魂，执行层向 DeerFlow 学，交付层向 MiroFish 学，沉淀层则比它们都更重。</p>
+      <div class="fusion">
+        <div class="fusion-col">
+          <h3>Access Plane</h3>
+          <div class="layer l1">IM / Web / API / Operator entry</div>
+          <div class="layer l1">Unified request normalization</div>
+          <div class="borrow keep"><strong>Keep from OpenFons:</strong> all user requests must enter one unified compilation chain, not separate ad-hoc paths.</div>
+        </div>
+        <div class="fusion-col">
+          <h3>Control Plane</h3>
+          <div class="layer l2">Intent Structuring</div>
+          <div class="layer l2">Planning Swarm</div>
+          <div class="layer l2">Opportunity Judge</div>
+          <div class="layer l2">OpportunitySpec -&gt; TaskSpec -&gt; WorkflowSpec</div>
+          <div class="borrow keep"><strong>Keep from OpenFons:</strong> this is the irreplaceable differentiator. It decides whether the system should execute at all.</div>
+        </div>
+        <div class="fusion-col">
+          <h3>Execution Plane</h3>
+          <div class="layer l3">Runtime harness</div>
+          <div class="layer l3">Skills loader and tools registry</div>
+          <div class="layer l3">Subagent executor</div>
+          <div class="layer l3">Provider adapters and gateway</div>
+          <div class="borrow deer"><strong>Borrow from DeerFlow:</strong> modular runtime, model/provider abstraction, agent/task execution infrastructure.</div>
+        </div>
+        <div class="fusion-col">
+          <h3>Data and Delivery Plane</h3>
+          <div class="layer l4">Topic / TopicRun / Evidence / EvidenceSet / Artifact</div>
+          <div class="layer l4">ReportSpec and report-web</div>
+          <div class="layer l4">Process timeline, evidence cards, graph/report visualization</div>
+          <div class="borrow miro"><strong>Borrow from MiroFish:</strong> productized output, visible process, graph/report narrative, strong result pages.</div>
+        </div>
+      </div>
+      <div class="callout-row">
+        <div class="callout">
+          <h4>Preserve</h4>
+          <ul class="flat">
+            <li>OpportunitySpec as the only upstream truth for downstream execution.</li>
+            <li>EvidenceSet as durable asset, not transient run output.</li>
+            <li>ReportSpec as delivery contract, not just a render hint.</li>
+          </ul>
+        </div>
+        <div class="callout">
+          <h4>Import</h4>
+          <ul class="flat">
+            <li>DeerFlow-style harness and adapter boundaries.</li>
+            <li>MiroFish-style final experience and report storytelling.</li>
+            <li>Provider policy as a first-class architectural object.</li>
+          </ul>
+        </div>
+        <div class="callout">
+          <h4>Avoid</h4>
+          <ul class="flat">
+            <li>One mega-agent controlling everything.</li>
+            <li>Thread/memory state replacing structured evidence truth.</li>
+            <li>Simulation-heavy complexity entering v1 mainline too early.</li>
+          </ul>
+        </div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+
+    <div class="section">
+      <h2>5. Recommended Process Flow for OpenFons</h2>
+      <p class="lead">下面这条流程，才是三者融合后真正适合 OpenFons 的版本。它不是“接到问题直接搜”，而是先判定价值、再预算、再执行、再沉淀、再交付。</p>
+      <div class="flow-grid">
+        <div class="step"><strong>1. User Input</strong>Free-form question, topic, task request, or opportunity seed.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>2. Intent Structuring</strong>Normalize audience, geo, language, intent, market, and desired output.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>3. Planning Swarm</strong>Demand, competition, monetization, and feasibility analysis.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>4. Opportunity Judge</strong>Decide whether the topic is worth execution and public delivery.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>5. OpportunitySpec</strong>Freeze the chosen opportunity and expose one upstream contract.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>6. Task and Workflow Compile</strong>Translate opportunity into execution budget, tools, workers, and evidence requirements.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>7. Controlled Collection</strong>Run skills, workers, subagents, and adapters under policy constraints.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>8. Evidence Assetization</strong>Store normalized evidence, provenance, artifacts, and versioned outputs.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>9. ReportSpec and Rendering</strong>Generate report structure and render polished report-web outputs.</div>
+        <div class="step"><strong>10. Reuse and Expansion</strong>Reuse evidence and opportunity assets for follow-up pages, reports, and long-term watches.</div>
+      </div>
+      <div class="footer">
+        This is the key shift from DeerFlow and MiroFish:
+        they are both closer to "execute and output";
+        OpenFons should become "judge, execute, retain, and compound".
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  </div>
+</body>
+</html>
*** End Patch

Three Project Architectures\" [level=2] [ref=e22]\n    - paragraph [ref=e23]: 这三列不是按代码量分，而是按“系统重心”分。你会看到：MiroFish 重在产品表达，DeerFlow 重在运行时组织，OpenFons 的目标则应该重在控制层 + 沉淀层。\n    - generic [ref=e24]:\n      - text: User input from IM, Web, API, or operator workflows.\n          - generic [ref=e63]:\n            - strong [ref=e64]: Control Plane\n            - text: Intent Structuring, Planning Swarm, Opportunity Judge, OpportunitySpec, Task Compiler.\n          - generic [ref=e65]:\n            - strong [ref=e66]: Execution Plane\n            - text: Skills, workers, controlled collection, source registry, policy gates.\n          - generic [ref=e67]:\n            - strong [ref=e68]: Data and Delivery Plane\n            - text: Topic, Evidence, EvidenceSet, Artifact, ReportSpec, report-web.\n        - generic [ref=e69]:\n          - generic [ref=e70]: \"Core difference: execution is not the final goal; evidence and artifacts are first-class assets.\"\n          - generic [ref=e71]: \"Core risk today: execution-layer modularity and product-level delivery experience are both not yet mature enough.\"\n          - generic [ref=e72]: \"Core mission: keep the control plane, strengthen runtime, and elevate delivery.\"\n  - generic [ref=e73]:\n    - heading \"3. What Each One Is Really Best At\" [level=2] [ref=e74]\n    - paragraph [ref=e75]: 这部分回答一个关键问题：如果不把系统拆层，只讲“整体谁更好”，很容易失真。正确问法应该是“哪一层借谁”。\n    - generic [ref=e76]:\n      - generic [ref=e78]: MiroFish\n      - generic [ref=e79]: DeerFlow\n      - generic [ref=e80]: OpenFons Should Become\n      - generic [ref=e81]: Best Role\n      - generic [ref=e82]: Vertical product shell\n      - generic [ref=e83]: Horizontal execution runtime\n      - generic [ref=e84]: Source-native research and publishing platform\n      - generic [ref=e85]: Strongest Layer\n      - generic [ref=e86]: Delivery and product presentation\n      - generic [ref=e87]: Execution and orchestration\n      - generic [ref=e88]: Control plane plus long-term evidence assetization\n      - generic [ref=e89]: Weakest Layer\n      - generic [ref=e90]: General-purpose decoupled execution substrate\n      - generic [ref=e91]: Evidence-first control and asset persistence\n      - generic [ref=e92]: Runtime modularity and mature productized output UX\n      - generic [ref=e93]: Borrow Target\n      - generic [ref=e94]: Report UX, graph/report narrative, process visibility\n      - generic [ref=e95]: Skills, providers, subagents, gateway, harness\n      - generic [ref=e96]: Keep OpportunitySpec, EvidenceSet, ReportSpec as SoT\n      - generic [ref=e97]: Not To Copy\n      - generic [ref=e98]: AGPL code path and simulation-heavy core flow\n      - generic [ref=e99]: Runtime state as final source of truth\n      - generic [ref=e100]: Do not collapse into one giant all-purpose agent\n  - generic [ref=e101]:\n    - 'heading \"4. Fusion Architecture: Recommended Best Practice\" [level=2] [ref=e102]'\n    - paragraph [ref=e103]: 最优融合方案不是“把两个仓库拼起来”，而是分层取长补短。控制层保留 OpenFons 自己的灵魂，执行层向 DeerFlow 学，交付层向 MiroFish 学，沉淀层则比它们都更重。\n    - generic [ref=e104]:\n      - generic [ref=e105]:\n        - heading \"Access Plane\" [level=3] [ref=e106]\n        - generic [ref=e107]: IM / Web / API / Operator entry\n        - generic [ref=e108]: Unified request normalization\n        - generic [ref=e109]:\n          - strong [ref=e110]: \"Keep from OpenFons:\"\n          - text: all user requests must enter one unified compilation chain, not separate ad-hoc paths.\n      - generic [ref=e111]:\n        - heading \"Control Plane\" [level=3] [ref=e112]\n        - generic [ref=e113]: Intent Structuring\n        - generic [ref=e114]: Planning Swarm\n        - generic [ref=e115]: Opportunity Judge\n        - generic [ref=e116]: OpportunitySpec -> TaskSpec -> WorkflowSpec\n        - generic [ref=e117]:\n          - strong [ref=e118]: \"Keep from OpenFons:\"\n          - text: this is the irreplaceable differentiator. It decides whether the system should execute at all.\n      - generic [ref=e119]:\n        - heading \"Execution Plane\" [level=3] [ref=e120]\n        - generic [ref=e121]: Runtime harness\n        - generic [ref=e122]: Skills loader and tools registry\n        - generic [ref=e123]: Subagent executor\n        - generic [ref=e124]: Provider adapters and gateway\n        - generic [ref=e125]:\n          - strong [ref=e126]: \"Borrow from DeerFlow:\"\n          - text: modular runtime, model/provider abstraction, agent/task execution infrastructure.\n      - generic [ref=e127]:\n        - heading \"Data and Delivery Plane\" [level=3] [ref=e128]\n        - generic [ref=e129]: Topic / TopicRun / Evidence / EvidenceSet / Artifact\n        - generic [ref=e130]: ReportSpec and report-web\n        - generic [ref=e131]: Process timeline, evidence cards, graph/report visualization\n        - generic [ref=e132]:\n          - strong [ref=e133]: \"Borrow from MiroFish:\"\n          - text: productized output, visible process, graph/report narrative, strong result pages.\n    - generic [ref=e134]:\n      - generic [ref=e135]:\n        - heading \"Preserve\" [level=4] [ref=e136]\n        - list [ref=e137]:\n          - listitem [ref=e138]: OpportunitySpec as the only upstream truth for downstream execution.\n          - listitem [ref=e139]: EvidenceSet as durable asset, not transient run output.\n          - listitem [ref=e140]: ReportSpec as delivery contract, not just a render hint.\n      - generic [ref=e141]:\n        - heading \"Import\" [level=4] [ref=e142]\n        - list [ref=e143]:\n          - listitem [ref=e144]: DeerFlow-style harness and adapter boundaries.\n          - listitem [ref=e145]: MiroFish-style final experience and report storytelling.\n          - listitem [ref=e146]: Provider policy as a first-class architectural object.\n      - generic [ref=e147]:\n        - heading \"Avoid\" [level=4] [ref=e148]\n        - list [ref=e149]:\n          - listitem [ref=e150]: One mega-agent controlling everything.\n          - listitem [ref=e151]: Thread/memory state replacing structured evidence truth.\n          - listitem [ref=e152]: Simulation-heavy complexity entering v1 mainline too early.\n  - generic [ref=e153]:\n    - heading \"5. Recommended Process Flow for OpenFons\" [level=2] [ref=e154]\n    - paragraph [ref=e155]: 下面这条流程，才是三者融合后真正适合 OpenFons 的版本。它不是“接到问题直接搜”，而是先判定价值、再预算、再执行、再沉淀、再交付。\n    - generic [ref=e156]:\n      - generic [ref=e157]:\n        - strong [ref=e158]: 1. User Input\n        - text: Free-form question, topic, task request, or opportunity seed.\n      - generic [ref=e159]:\n        - strong [ref=e160]: 2. Intent Structuring\n        - text: Normalize audience, geo, language, intent, market, and desired output.\n      - generic [ref=e161]:\n        - strong [ref=e162]: 3. Planning Swarm\n        - text: Demand, competition, monetization, and feasibility analysis.\n      - generic [ref=e163]:\n        - strong [ref=e164]: 4. Opportunity Judge\n        - text: Decide whether the topic is worth execution and public delivery.\n      - generic [ref=e165]:\n        - strong [ref=e166]: 5. OpportunitySpec\n        - text: Freeze the chosen opportunity and expose one upstream contract.\n      - generic [ref=e167]:\n        - strong [ref=e168]: 6. Task and Workflow Compile\n        - text: Translate opportunity into execution budget, tools, workers, and evidence requirements.\n      - generic [ref=e169]:\n        - strong [ref=e170]: 7. Controlled Collection\n        - text: Run skills, workers, subagents, and adapters under policy constraints.\n      - generic [ref=e171]:\n        - strong [ref=e172]: 8. Evidence Assetization\n        - text: Store normalized evidence, provenance, artifacts, and versioned outputs.\n      - generic [ref=e173]:\n        - strong [ref=e174]: 9. ReportSpec and Rendering\n        - text: Generate report structure and render polished report-web outputs.\n      - generic [ref=e175]:\n        - strong [ref=e176]: 10. Reuse and Expansion\n        - text: Reuse evidence and opportunity assets for follow-up pages, reports, and long-term watches.\n    - generic [ref=e177]: \"This is the key shift from DeerFlow and MiroFish: they are both closer to \\\"execute and output\\\"; OpenFons should become \\\"judge, execute, retain, and compound\\\".\"\n```\n### Events\n- New console entries: ..\\..\\Temp\\playwright-mcp-output\\1774579396056\\console-2026-03-27T02-43-16-331Z.log#L1\n- [ERROR] Failed to load resource: the server respon...04 (Not Found) @ http://localhost:49948/favicon.ico:0"
  }
